title: Video fingerprinting based on fourier transform of histogram
abstract: A content device and method is disclosed to include a processing device to process streaming video content. A fingerprinter receives captured frames of the streaming video content and, for each frame of a plurality of the captured frames, generates a one-dimensional histogram function of pixel values and transforms the histogram function with a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), to generate a plurality of complex values for the frame. The fingerprinter further, for each of the plurality of complex values, assigns a binary one ("1") when a real part of the complex value is greater than zero ("0") and assigns a binary zero ("0") when the real part is less than or equal to zero, to generate a plurality of bits. The fingerprinter further concatenates a specific number of the bits to generate a fingerprint for the frame.
claims:
Claims Claims  What is claimed is: 1. A content device comprising: a processing device to process streaming video content; a capture interface including a port through which to receive the streaming video content; a fingerprinter, executable by the processing device, to receive captured frames of the streaming video content and to, for each frame of a plurality of the captured frames: generate a one-dimensional histogram function of pixel values; transform the histogram function with a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), to generate a plurality of complex values for the frame; for each of the plurality of complex values, assign a binary one ("1") when a real part of the complex value is greater than zero ("0") and assign a binary zero ("0") when the real part is less than or equal to zero, to generate a plurality of bits; and concatenate a specific number of the plurality of bits to generate a fingerprint for the frame that can be compared with another fingerprint to identify the streaming video content; and a memory operatively coupled to the processing device and in which to store the captured frames and the fingerprint. 2. The content device of claim 1, wherein the fingerprinter is further to generate a second fingerprint for the plurality of captured frames by concatenating the fingerprint of each frame of at least some of the plurality of captured frames. 3. The content device of claim 1, further comprising a capturer executable by the processing device, the capturer to capture frames of the streaming video content from the capture interface and to provide the plurality of captured frames to the fingerprinter. 4. The content device of claim 1, wherein the histogram function includes an x axis representing specific pixel values and a y axis representing a number of pixels at each respective specific pixel value. 5. The content device of claim 4, wherein the fingerprinter is further to use a Y value of YUV space as a pixel value of each captured frame to generate each respective histogram function. 6. The content device of claim 1, wherein the fingerprinter is further to index the plurality of complex values from zero ("0") to 255. 7. The content device of claim 1, wherein the fingerprint is 128 bits long or 256 bits long. 8. The content device of claim 1, wherein the fingerprinter is further to transmit the fingerprint to an automatic content recognition cloud (ACR) server to be stored in relation to corresponding broadcast schedule metadata. 9. A method comprising: receiving, by a fingerprinter executed by a processing device and from a capturer interface of a content device, a plurality of captured frames of streaming video content to be processed; generating, by the fingerprinter and for a frame of the plurality of captured frames, a one- dimensional histogram function of pixel values; transforming, by the fingerprinter, the histogram function with a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), to generate a plurality of complex values for the frame; for each of the plurality of complex values, assigning a binary one ("1") when a real part of the complex value is greater than zero ("0") and assigning a binary zero ("0") when the real part is less than or equal to zero, to generate a plurality of bits; and concatenating, by the fingerprinter, a specific number of the plurality of bits to generate a fingerprint for the frame that can be compared with another fingerprint to identify the streaming video content. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising generating a second fingerprint, by the fingerprinter, for the plurality of captured frames by concatenating a fingerprint of every other frame of the plurality of captured frames. 11. The method of claim 9, further comprising capturing, by a capturer executed by the processing device, the streaming video content and providing, by the capturer, the plurality of captured frames to the fingerprinter. 12. The method of claim 9, wherein the histogram function includes an x axis representing specific pixel values and a y axis representing a number of pixels at each respective specific pixel value. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising using, by the fingerprinter, a Y value of YUV space as a pixel value of each captured frame to generate each respective histogram function. 14. The method of claim 9, further comprising indexing, by the fingerprinter, the plurality of complex values from zero ("0") to 255. 15. The method of claim 9, wherein the fingerprint is 128 bits long or 256 bits long. 16. The method of claim 9, further comprising transmitting the fingerprint to an automatic content recognition cloud (ACR) server to be stored in relation to corresponding broadcast schedule metadata. 17. A non-transitory machine-readable storage medium having instructions that, when executed by a processing device, cause the processing device to: receive, by a fingerprinter executed by the processing device and from a capturer interface, a plurality of captured frames of streaming video content to be processed; generate, by the fingerprinter and for a frame of the plurality of captured frames, a one- dimensional histogram function of pixel values; transform, by the fingerprinter, the histogram function with a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), to generate a plurality of complex values for the frame; for each of the plurality of complex values, assign a binary one ("1") when a real part of the complex value is greater than zero ("0") and assign a binary zero ("0") when the real part is less than or equal to zero, to generate a plurality of bits; and 

 concatenate, by the fingerprinter, a specific number of the plurality of bits to generate a fingerprint for the frame that can be compared with another fingerprint to identify the streaming video content. 18. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processing device, are further to cause the fingerprinter to generate a second fingerprint for the plurality of captured frames by concatenating the fingerprint of each frame of at least some of the plurality of captured frames. 19. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processing device, are further to cause a capturer to capture frames of the streaming video content and to provide the plurality of captured frames to the fingerprinter. 20. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the histogram function includes an x axis representing specific pixel values and a y axis representing a number of pixels at each respective specific pixel value. 21. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 20, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processing device, are further to cause the fingerprinter to use a Y value of YUV space as a pixel value of each captured frame to generate each respective histogram function. 22. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processing device, are further to cause the fingerprinter to index the plurality of complex values from zero ("0") to 255. 23. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processing device, are further to cause the fingerprinter to transmit the fingerprint to an automatic content recognition cloud (ACR) server to be stored in relation to corresponding broadcast schedule metadata.
